mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict short-term equity returns during periods of supply chain disruption or inventory drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5% signals economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize in >55% of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth exceeds 400k while unemployment remains flat, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings paired with a rising federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation and rate hikes, or the negative signal fails to predict 5-day returns in >65% of tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production correlates with higher corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production trends show no statistical relationship with 20-day equity returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains negative.
